Stucco Repair in Bell County, TX

Bell County homes and commercial buildings face a demanding mix of summer heat, strong thunderstorms, and shifting soils that take a toll on stucco surfaces. Star Stucco of Austin brings professional repair services to Temple, Killeen, Belton, and the communities in between, fixing everything from hairline cracks to major water damage.

Get a Free EstimateCall (512) 706-9699

How Bell County Weather Affects Your Stucco

Bell County sits at the northern edge of the Central Texas corridor, where the Blackland Prairie meets the limestone hills to the west. The region's mix of clay and rocky soils creates uneven foundation movement that puts constant stress on stucco walls, especially around windows, doors, and where different building materials meet.

Summers bring extended stretches of triple-digit heat followed by severe afternoon thunderstorms. That rapid temperature swing causes stucco to expand and contract repeatedly, opening hairline cracks that may look minor but can let significant amounts of water into the wall during the next downpour.

Military families stationed at Fort Cavazos and homeowners throughout Killeen, Harker Heights, and Temple trust us to keep their stucco in solid condition. We understand the pace of life here and work efficiently to minimize disruption while delivering repairs that stand up to the local climate for years.

What's Included With Our Stucco Repair in Bell County

Every project includes expert craftsmanship, quality materials, and a commitment to lasting results.

Thorough Damage Assessment

We examine every wall of your property, documenting cracks, soft spots, staining, and areas where the stucco sounds hollow or has separated from the substrate.

Moisture Testing

We probe behind the stucco at all suspect areas to measure moisture levels and determine whether water has reached the sheathing or framing.

Removal of Compromised Material

Damaged stucco is carefully cut away until we reach sound, well-bonded material. This ensures the repair bonds solidly rather than sitting on top of a weak layer.

Substrate Restoration

We repair or replace lath, building paper, and any sheathing that has been compromised by water or structural movement.

Color & Texture Matching

Our team samples your existing stucco and creates a custom mix that matches the color and replicates the texture of your original finish.

Final Finish & Protective Seal

We apply the finish coat, match the texture, and seal the surface to protect against the UV exposure and rain cycles that Bell County exteriors face.

Common Stucco Repair Needs in Bell County

These are the stucco problems we see most often on Bell County homes. Each situation requires a targeted approach to produce a lasting repair.

Window & Door Cracking

Openings are the weakest points in any stucco wall, and foundation movement in Bell County soils causes cracks to radiate from window and door corners. We rebuild these areas with reinforced mesh and flexible materials.

Water Damage Repair

Heavy rain pushes water through cracks and failed caulk joints. We trace every entry point, dry the wall cavity, and rebuild the stucco with proper flashing and drainage to prevent future leaks.

Failing Patch Repairs

Patches applied by previous contractors without proper surface prep or bonding agents break loose quickly. We remove the old patch, prepare the substrate, and apply a durable replacement.

Stucco Delamination

When the stucco layer separates from the lath behind it, it eventually cracks and falls away. We secure or replace the affected area and restore the wall to its original strength.

Efflorescence & Dark Staining

Mineral deposits and mold-related discoloration indicate ongoing moisture movement through the wall. We address the moisture source and restore the clean appearance of the stucco.

Storm & Impact Damage

Bell County sees frequent severe weather including large hail. We repair gouges, chips, and dents caused by hailstones, flying debris, and accidental impact from yard equipment.

Why Choose Us for Stucco Repair in Bell County?

  • We trace every crack and stain back to its root cause so the repair addresses the real problem, not just the visible symptom.
  • Our materials are selected to handle the thermal cycling and soil conditions specific to Bell County.
  • Color and texture matching on every job means your repaired wall looks uniform, not patched.
  • Substrate and moisture issues are resolved before new stucco goes on, eliminating the most common reason repairs fail.
  • We stand behind our work with a written warranty on materials and labor.
  • Free inspections are available across Bell County — schedule yours at (512) 706-9699.

Licensed & Insured

Full liability coverage and workers' compensation for every project in Bell County.

20+ Years Experience

Decades of hands-on stucco work across Central Texas residential and commercial properties.

Certified Applicators

Certified by Sto Corp, Senergy/BASF, Master Wall, Dryvit, and Parex LaHabra for the highest quality results.

Our Stucco Repair Process

A straightforward approach from start to finish — no surprises, no shortcuts.

1

Request an Inspection

Reach out by phone or through our website to set up a visit. We serve Bell County from Temple and Killeen to Belton and the surrounding areas.

2

Diagnose the Problem

Our technician examines the stucco, tests for moisture behind the surface, and identifies whether soil movement, water intrusion, or material age is driving the damage.

3

Review the Repair Proposal

We present an itemized plan that covers every step of the repair along with its cost. You will know exactly what we are going to do and what it will cost before we start.

4

Complete the Repair

Our team removes the damaged stucco, restores the substrate, and applies a sealed, color-matched finish. We clean up and walk the completed work with you.
